input.prompt:
I have a .NET 10 ASP.NET Core web app with:
- TargetFramework set to net10.0 in the csproj
- global.json pinning SDK to 10.0.100
- A Dockerfile using mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/aspnet:10.0 and sdk:10.0
How do I update everything for .NET 11?

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET 10 ASP.NET Core web app with:
- TargetFramework set to net10.0 in the csproj
- global.json pinning SDK to 10.0.100
- A Dockerfile using mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/aspnet:10.0 and sdk:10.0
How do I update everything for .NET 11?

data.content:
Update these three files:

**1. `.csproj`**
```xml
<!-- Change -->
<TargetFramework>net10.0</TargetFramework>
<!-- To -->
<TargetFramework>net11.0</TargetFramework>
```

**2. `global.json`**
```json
"sdk": { "version": "11.0.100" }
```

**3. `Dockerfile`**
```dockerfile
# Update base images
mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/aspnet:11.0
mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:11.0
```

**Then:**
```bash
dotnet --version          # verify SDK 11.0.100+ is installed
dotnet restore            # pick up any updated packages
dotnet build              # confirm it compiles
dotnet test               # run tests
```

Also check for any NuGet packages with version `10.0.x` (e.g. `Microsoft.AspNetCore.*`, `Microsoft.Extensions.*`) and bump them to their `11.0.x` equivalents. Review the [.NET 11 breaking changes](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/compatibility/11.0) for anything that affects your app.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking how to update their .NET project from .NET 10 to .NET 11. This is a straightforward guidance question — I don't need to search any codebase since they're describing their setup. Let me provide concise instructions.
